summary:
A critical strategy meeting for AnswerAI (AAI) with all three principals. The SBI Builder deal still hasn't closed — Brad followed up with Pedro who said no response yet. With AAI's bank account at ~$29K and $13K just sent out, runway is effectively less than one month with a $20-25K burn rate (Max, Bisma, Diego). Last Rev's runway is 3-4 months with current costs. The team confronted the reality that both companies cannot survive past 3-6 months without changes. WOW ($17K/month, high margin, low effort) is at risk due to acquisition and zero-line budgeting. Diligent has been the lifeline but work is winding down in 4-6 weeks.

The team aligned on pivoting Answer Engine away from the generalized platform/builder approach toward focused, specific use cases: domain research, content ingestion, tagging, JIRA analysis, SEO analysis, and call transcript analysis — marketed as individual mini-apps with credits-based pricing. Brad presented a detailed pricing spreadsheet with credit tiers (Starter $30/2000 credits, Professional $50/3500 credits, etc.) and scenario calculators. The consensus was clear: the SaaS builder model is dead (stocks of Salesforce, ServiceNow down 30%), and they must sell specific outcomes, not features.

The most urgent priority agreed upon was lead generation. Max pushed hard for outbound email campaigns, landing pages per use case, and leveraging existing customer success stories (WOW, IAS, SBI). Brad demonstrated his OpenClaw-based lead gen pipeline (Legion) and content pipeline running on secure AWS instances. The team agreed: Monday after IAS 2.0 deployment, Max goes full-time on outbound marketing with Brad. Everyone not doing billable work goes hourly. A revenue-share model (up to 70%) was proposed for team members who build and launch mini-apps. The team also discussed Max's ownership stake in Last Rev — options included hourly + percentage or reduced base + equity, with the understanding that AAI funding from Last Rev must stop.
keyDiscussionPoints: AAI runway less than 1 month (~$16K remaining after recent payout), burn rate $20-25K/month
SBI Builder deal still not closed despite concessions — make or break for AAI
WOW $17K/month contract at risk due to acquisition (zero-line budgeting)
Pivot from generalized platform to focused Answer Engine use cases (domain research, tagging, ingestion, analysis)
Credits-based pricing model with tiered plans presented
Lead generation identified as #1 urgent priority
OpenClaw-based lead gen (Legion) and content pipeline demonstrated by Brad
Secure AWS deployment for OpenClaw instances — $12/month per person, Okta SSO
Everyone goes hourly if SBI doesn't close — billable hours required
70% revenue share proposed for team members building/launching mini-apps
Max ownership discussion: 10% + hourly or reduced base (~25% cut) + equity
SaaS market declining — need growth metrics for potential VC funding
Fire Crawl replacing EXA for domain research (11 seconds vs 3 minutes, much cheaper)
IAS paying $15K/month, Bisma could be dedicated to IAS for better margins
Marketing website needs overhaul — remove builder/FlowWise, focus on Answer Engine apps
decisions: Lead generation is the #1 priority starting next week
Pivot Answer Engine marketing to individual use-case apps (domain research, call analysis, etc.)
Everyone not doing billable work goes to hourly
Stop funding AAI from Last Rev — AAI must be self-sufficient
Bisma to be potentially dedicated to IAS account
Diego goes hourly if SBI doesn't close
Marketing website to be overhauled focusing on Answer Engine use cases
Revenue share up to 70% for mini-app creators
Max and Brad to focus on outbound marketing after IAS 2.0 deployment
Fire Crawl adopted over EXA for domain research (cost and speed improvement)

actionItems.action: Deploy IAS 2.0 and close remaining items
Set up OpenClaw content pipeline for Max
Create marketing landing pages per use case (domain research, call analysis, etc.)
Overhaul Answer Engine marketing website — remove builder/FlowWise content
Finalize credits-based pricing model and Stripe integration
Create demo video for Answer Engine use cases
Have Krista draft Diligent added-scope document for next SOW
Prepare all-hands presentation on OpenClaw deployment for Tuesday
Merge Last Rev marketing website cleanup PR (135 files changed)

followUp: SBI Builder — final answer needed ASAP
